name: Portobello Mushroom Burgers With
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups mesquite wood chips, soaked in cold water for 1 hour (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the dressing ingredients (mayonnaise, chopped basil, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, olive oil, and minced garlic) in a small bowl.
2. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
3. In another small bowl, whisk together olive oil and minced garlic.
4. Prepare the barbecue on medium-high heat. When the coals are white-hot, drain the soaked wood chips (if using) and scatter them over the coals.
5. Brush the mushroom caps on both sides with the garlic oil mixture, seasoning with salt and pepper.
6. Grill the mushroom caps until tender and golden brown, about 4 minutes per side. Transfer to a platter and cover with foil to keep warm.
7. Grill the cut sides of the hamburger buns until light golden, about 2 minutes.
8. Place the bottom half of each burger bun on plates. Top each with a mushroom cap, lettuce leaf, and tomato slice.
9. Spoon some basil-mustard sauce over the tomatoes and top with the bun.
10. Pass remaining basil-mustard sauce separately.
